reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
Monday in late June. Nothing special on the calendar. The sun's been up since before five, and the week stretches ahead with its meetings and grocery runs and the small obligations that fill an actual life. This is the part nobody warned me about when I got sober — not the cravings or the hard nights, but the sheer ordinariness of a Tuesday becoming a Wednesday becoming a year.

When I was drinking, I treated ordinary like an insult. A regular Monday meant something was missing, and I'd manufacture the missing thing by the time the sun went down. What I couldn't see then was that I was burning the foundation I needed to build anything real on. The trips, the friendships, the early mornings on a chairlift, the quiet competence of knowing what you'll do today — none of that grows out of chaos. It grows out of Mondays.

Durant was paraphrasing Aristotle, and the point lands the same either way: the life you want isn't waiting on the other side of some big decision. It's being assembled, quietly, in what you do today. Make the bed. Answer the text. Go to the meeting or the gym or just outside. The ordinary is not the consolation prize for getting sober. It's the prize.
reflection.meditation: What is one small, ordinary thing I can do today that the drinking version of me would have skipped? Do that thing, and notice you did it.

affirmations: Today I am building my life in the small choices, not waiting for a big one.
I am someone who shows up — for the boring parts, not just the exciting ones.
I have agency over how this day goes, and I'm using it.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for an ordinary Monday I get to be awake for. Grateful for a body that works, a mind that's clear enough to plan the week, and people who know my real name and my real number. None of this was guaranteed, and I haven't forgotten the years I spent unable to access any of it.
gratitude.closing: One more day, clear-eyed. That's the whole thing.
